hero-tagline: A prominent civil society movement uniting students, professionals, activists, and community leaders to promote peace, justice, and global awareness for Palestine.
intro-p1: <0>Pak-Palestine Forum (PPF)</0> is a prominent civil society movement in Pakistan dedicated to advocacy, humanitarian support, and public mobilization for the rights of the Palestinian people. Originally founded as the <1>Save Gaza Campaign</1>, the forum unites students, professionals, activists, and community leaders on a non-political platform to promote peace, justice, and international awareness regarding Palestine.
intro-p2: The organization works nationwide to organize awareness campaigns, humanitarian initiatives, and solidarity activities aimed at strengthening global support for Palestine.
vision-h2: Vision and Mission of Pak-Palestine Forum
vision-p: Pak-Palestine Forum envisions a just world where human dignity, peace, and international accountability prevail. The forum's mission focuses on uniting Pakistani civil society to advocate for ceasefire efforts, humanitarian relief, and global awareness regarding the Palestinian cause.
vision-obj-0: Promote public awareness about humanitarian crises in Palestine.
vision-obj-1: Advocate for peaceful solutions and international accountability.
vision-obj-4: Build a unified platform for civic engagement.
vision-footer: The forum consists of diverse members including students, educators, professionals, and community leaders working together for advocacy and humanitarian action.
impact-p: From grassroots mobilization to international representation, Pak-Palestine Forum has made a lasting impact in advocating for the Palestinian cause across Pakistan and beyond.
impact-desc-0: Successfully led large-scale rallies, protests, and solidarity events across Pakistan, drawing massive public participation in support of Palestine.
impact-desc-1: Built a strong grassroots network of volunteers across cities, towns, and universities, enabling sustained advocacy and on-ground humanitarian support.
impact-desc-2: Conducted continuous campaigns through social media, public events, educational sessions, and media engagement to keep the Palestinian cause at the forefront of national consciousness.
impact-stat-3: Represented Pakistan in Global Solidarity Platforms
impact-desc-3: Participated in international conferences, forums, and solidarity networks, bringing the Pakistani civil society's voice to global platforms supporting Palestine.
impact-desc-4: Members have actively participated in global solidarity initiatives, including international flotillas and aid convoys, reflecting PPF's commitment to direct humanitarian action beyond advocacy.
impact-desc-5: Organized some of Pakistan's largest Palestine solidarity marches and public gatherings, mobilizing hundreds of thousands of citizens to stand in support of Palestinian rights.
impact-desc-6: Played a significant role in keeping the Palestine issue prominent in Pakistan's mainstream media, contributing to informed public opinion and increased political accountability.
presence-p: Pak-Palestine Forum operates through volunteer networks across major cities. The organization's decentralized model allows widespread civic participation nationwide.
faq-h2: Frequently Asked Questions About Pak-Palestine Forum
faq-0-a: Pak-Palestine Forum is a civil society organization in Pakistan advocating for humanitarian support, peace, and awareness regarding Palestine.
faq-1-a: It emerged from the Save Gaza Campaign, a nationwide movement organizing public mobilization and awareness initiatives.
faq-2-a: No, it operates as a non-political civic platform focused on humanitarian advocacy.
faq-3-a: People can volunteer, participate in awareness campaigns, support relief activities, or collaborate professionally.
faq-4-q: What impact does the forum create?
faq-4-a: The forum organizes nationwide protests, advocacy campaigns, and humanitarian initiatives supporting Palestine.
team-h2: Leadership & Core Team of Pak-Palestine Forum
team-0-bio: Wahaj Ahmed serves as the leader of Pak-Palestine Forum and plays a key role in guiding the organization's advocacy strategy, awareness campaigns, and nationwide mobilization efforts. He has publicly addressed national audiences on the forum's objectives, emphasizing human dignity, solidarity, and collective civic engagement.
team-1-bio: Former Senator Mushtaq Ahmad Khan is a senior political figure and patron of the forum. He has led major solidarity campaigns, public protests, and advocacy initiatives demanding humanitarian action and international accountability regarding Palestine.
team-2-role: Central Team Member & Founder of Save Gaza Campaign
team-2-bio: Humaira Tayyaba is a social activist and founder of the Save Gaza Campaign, which later evolved into Pak-Palestine Forum. She has led awareness campaigns, public briefings, and national advocacy initiatives focused on humanitarian support and global awareness regarding Palestine.
join-p: Pak-Palestine Forum welcomes volunteers, professionals, and organizations to contribute to humanitarian and advocacy initiatives.
join-footer: The forum emphasizes collective civic engagement and voluntary participation to create meaningful impact.
